summary: Backend + Frontend regression for Atlas Core 2.5D radial HUD (3 concentric rings with drag-to-rotate). Backend: 10 API endpoints validated (9 pass + 1 pass after fixing .env parse bug). Frontend: 19/21 critical UI assertions pass. All 3 rings render, all 16 tiles present with correct testids, AI selection + active-ai glow + status update + side panel + FileBrowserPanel routing (memory/archive) all work. Drag-to-rotate visually confirmed via screenshot comparison. Voice mic button present and click does not crash.

minor.issue: Initially returned 503 because backend/.env had CORS_ORIGINS and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY on the SAME line ('CORS_ORIGINS="*"EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=...'). python-dotenv logged 'could not parse statement starting at line 3' and silently dropped EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. FIXED by splitting into two lines; chat endpoint now returns 200 with real LLM response.
Each route module creates its own AsyncIOMotorClient and calls load_dotenv() at import time. Works, but causes duplicate mongo clients + relies on implicit cwd for .env lookup. Low priority.
Review request referenced 'POST /api/chat/message' but the actual route is POST /api/chat/send. Update docs or add an alias.

ui_bugs.issue: No data-testid on root/overlay/close so it cannot be reliably queried in E2E. Modal DOES open on btn-upload click (verified via manual inspection), but test selectors [data-testid="file-upload-modal"] / [role="dialog"] return null.
No data-testid on root / close button. Works functionally (opens on memory + archive clicks, visible in screenshot) but harder to test.

At 1920px viewport, outer-ring side tiles are clipped ('SUBJECT' instead of 'SUBJECTS', 'YCLOPEDIA' instead of 'ENCYCLOPEDIA', 'LUEPRINTS' instead of 'BLUEPRINTS'). Text overflows its container when tiles are positioned near the edge of ring-stage. Consider increasing stage width, shrinking font, or adding tile padding.

After a pure drag (no click) that rotates the ring, no tile receives 'is-top'. 'is-top' appears to be bound to selectedId rather than geometric position. Review request expects drag-snap to 'fire onSelect' (so the new top tile gets is-top) — verify DialRing.js L67-92 actually calls onSelect on snap release, not only on click.

issues: Tile labels truncated on outer ring (see above)

Outer-ring square boundary (blue box from 620,200 to 1290,880 in screenshot) is visible — appears to be the ring-stage's debug/container border. Confirm this is intentional; reference image shows only circular rings with no square frame.

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/chat/send ajani → 200 with real LLM response (after .env fix)
Frontend: All 16 tiles present with correct data-testid ring-{layer}-{id}
Frontend: Inner ring click (minerva) adds active-ai + is-top classes, ajani loses active-ai
Frontend: Status text updates to MINERVA with teal color
Frontend: Side panel opens on AI select
Frontend: Middle ring 'memory' opens FileBrowserPanel (NOT side panel)
Frontend: btn-mic present (Web Speech supported in Chromium), click does not crash (no ReferenceError)
Frontend: btn-upload click dispatches (modal opens visually)
Frontend: Click-not-drag selects tile (lab click opens panel)
Frontend: Drag rotates outer ring (before-screenshot LAB at top, after-screenshot SUBJECTS at top)
action_items: Add data-testid to FileUploadModal (e.g. 'file-upload-modal' on root, 'file-upload-close' on close button).
Fix outer-ring tile label truncation (SUBJECTS/ENCYCLOPEDIA/BLUEPRINTS clipped). Either increase .dial-tile min-width, reduce font-size, or use text-overflow: ellipsis with a larger max-width.
Verify DialRing.js drag-release snap actually calls onSelect so the snapped top tile gets is-top / active state. Currently a rotation-only drag leaves no tile marked is-top.
Update review documentation: chat endpoint is POST /api/chat/send (not /api/chat/message).
Consider consolidating load_dotenv + Mongo client creation to server.py (currently duplicated in each route module).
critical_code_review_comments: backend/.env had malformed line (CORS_ORIGINS and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY concatenated) — silently broke all LLM calls. Fixed this iteration. Add a startup assertion: `assert os.environ.get('EMERGENT_LLM_KEY'), 'LLM key missing'` to fail fast next time.

routes/chat.py lines 21-22 use os.environ.get with defaults for MONGO_URL/DB_NAME. Per repo rules, env vars must not have defaults so missing config fails fast. Same pattern in routes/files.py L19-20.

HUDInterface.js click handler for outer tiles calls openSection synchronously; DialRing internal drag detection (>4° gesture) was not fully verified end-to-end from outside (no is-top applied after snap).
FileUploadModal and FileBrowserPanel lack data-testid on interactive elements — violates the stated testability rule in /app/memory (all interactive elements must have data-testid).
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (split CORS_ORIGINS and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY onto separate lines)

success_rate.frontend: 90% (19/21 critical assertions; 2 selector-related test issues, underlying features work)

seed_data_creation: Upload test created and deleted TEST_atlas.txt (cleanup included).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend .env must stay with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY on its own line. Chat endpoint is /api/chat/send. Frontend: DialRing 'is-top' class semantics need main-agent review — appears tied to selectedId, not geometric top after drag-only rotation. FileUploadModal/FileBrowserPanel lack data-testid; recommend adding before next UI regression. Outer-ring tile labels are clipped on wide viewports.
rca_of_the_issue: Chat 503: /app/backend/.env had two assignments on one line (`CORS_ORIGINS="*"EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=sk-emergent-...`). python-dotenv emitted 'could not parse statement starting at line 3' in backend stderr log and discarded EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Reproduction: `curl -X POST .../api/chat/send -d '{"persona":"ajani","message":"hi"}'` → HTTP 503 'AI services are currently offline'. Mitigation applied: rewrote .env with each KEY=VALUE on its own line and restarted backend via supervisorctl. Verified POST /api/chat/send now returns 200 with a real GPT-5.2 response.
